modelReleaseDate.notes: Claude 3.7 Sonnet was officially announced by Anthropic on February 24, 2025, as their most intelligent model to date and the first hybrid reasoning model on the market. The recorded date matches the official announcement date from Anthropic's news page.

Claude 3.7 Sonnet was officially announced by Anthropic on February 24, 2025. This is Anthropic's most intelligent model to date and the first hybrid reasoning model on the market. The announced date matches the provided date exactly. Note: There is no separate '16K' variant - Claude 3.7 Sonnet supports configurable thinking tokens up to its 128K output token limit.

Claude 3.7 Sonnet was officially announced by Anthropic on February 24, 2025. This is Anthropic's most intelligent model to date and the first hybrid reasoning model on the market. The announced date matches the provided date exactly. Note: There is no separate '16K' variant - Claude 3.7 Sonnet supports configurable thinking tokens up to its 128K output token limit.

Claude 3.7 Sonnet was officially announced by Anthropic on February 24, 2025. This is Anthropic's most intelligent model to date and the first hybrid reasoning model on the market. The announced date matches the provided date exactly. Note: There is no separate '16K' variant - Claude 3.7 Sonnet supports configurable thinking tokens up to its 128K output token limit.

Date corrected from 2025-02-15T00:00:00.000Z to 2024-02-15T00:00:00.000Z. Gemini 1.5 Pro was announced on February 15, 2024. The provided date of 2025-02-15 is incorrect (wrong year). The model was first publicly announced on Feb 15, 2024, with broader API availability starting April 9, 2024.

Date corrected from 2025-02-02T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-02-05T00:00:00.000Z. Gemini 2.0 Flash was initially released as experimental on December 11, 2024, and became generally available on February 5, 2025. The date provided (2025-02-02) is incorrect; the official GA release was February 5, 2025.

GPT-4.5 was officially announced and released by OpenAI on February 27, 2025. Multiple sources including CNBC, Axios, MIT Technology Review, and the official OpenAI announcement page confirm this date. The model was made available to ChatGPT Plus and Pro subscribers.

Date corrected from 2024-11-20T00:00:00.000Z to 2024-05-13T00:00:00.000Z. GPT-4o was officially announced by OpenAI on May 13, 2024, during their Spring Updates event. Multiple official sources (CNN, TechCrunch, and OpenAI's official announcement) confirm this date. The recorded date of November 20, 2024 is incorrect - that date may correspond to a different model update or feature release, but not the original GPT-4o release.
Official OpenAI blog post announcing GPT-4o mini on July 18, 2024. The model was released for developers via API and through ChatGPT web and mobile app on this date.

Date corrected from 2025-02-13T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-02-18T04:00:00.000Z. Grok 3 was released on February 17, 2025, at 8:00 PM PT (February 18, 2025, 4:00 AM UTC). The provided date of February 13 is incorrect by 5 days. Official announcement came from Elon Musk, with live demo event on the announced date.

Date corrected from 2025-02-13T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-02-17T00:00:00.000Z. Multiple sources including TechCrunch and xAI's official announcement confirm Grok 3 was released on February 17, 2025. The recorded date of February 13, 2025 is 4 days earlier than the actual release date.

Llama 3.3 70B was officially announced by Meta on December 6, 2024. Multiple sources confirm this date, including TechCrunch's coverage published on December 6, 2024, and official Meta announcements. The provided date matches the verified release announcement date.

OpenAI released the full o1 model to ChatGPT users on December 5, 2024, as part of their '12 Days of OpenAI' announcement campaign. This date matches the verified release date for the base o1 model, which applies to all variants including o1 (High).

OpenAI released the full o1 model to ChatGPT users on December 5, 2024, as part of their '12 Days of OpenAI' announcement campaign. This date matches the verified release date for the base o1 model, which applies to all variants including o1 (High).

OpenAI officially announced o1-pro (as part of the o1 pro mode feature in ChatGPT Pro) on December 5, 2024, during their '12 Days of OpenAI' announcement series. The o1-pro model is a version of o1 that uses additional compute resources for enhanced reasoning capabilities, available as part of the $200/month ChatGPT Pro subscription tier.

Official OpenAI blog announcement on September 12, 2024. The o1-preview model was made available to ChatGPT Plus and Team users, as well as through OpenAI's API on this date. Multiple official OpenAI sources and major tech news outlets (TechCrunch) confirm this release date.

OpenAI officially announced the o3 model (including the Preview variant with Low, Medium, and High reasoning levels) on December 20, 2024, during their '12 Days of OpenAI' event. The model was announced as a preview with early access applications accepted until January 10, 2025. The recorded release date of 2024-12-20T00:00:00.000Z matches the official announcement date.

OpenAI officially announced the o3 model (including the Preview variant with Low, Medium, and High reasoning levels) on December 20, 2024, during their '12 Days of OpenAI' event. The model was announced as a preview with early access applications accepted until January 10, 2025. The recorded release date of 2024-12-20T00:00:00.000Z matches the official announcement date.

Official release date confirmed by multiple sources including MIT Technology Review article dated January 31, 2025, and OpenAI's o3-mini System Card dated January 31, 2025. o3-mini was released to ChatGPT Plus, Team, and Pro users on this date, with free user access also becoming available.

Official release date confirmed by multiple sources including MIT Technology Review article dated January 31, 2025, and OpenAI's o3-mini System Card dated January 31, 2025. o3-mini was released to ChatGPT Plus, Team, and Pro users on this date, with free user access also becoming available.

The May 20, 2025 date appears to be when Google announced improvements to the model at Google I/O 2025, not the initial preview release date.

The provided date of May 20, 2025 is incorrect and predates the actual release by approximately 4 weeks.

Google officially announced Gemini 2.5 Pro (experimental) on March 25, 2025, on the official Google DeepMind blog. The experimental model identifier 'gemini-2.5-pro-exp-03-25' reflects the March 25 release date. Multiple sources confirm this date including 9to5Google, TechCrunch, and VentureBeat coverage from that date.

Official Meta announcement blog post confirms Llama 4 Maverick was announced and made available for download on April 5, 2025. The date provided matches the official announcement date from Meta's official AI blog.

GPT-4.1-Nano was officially announced and released by OpenAI on April 14, 2025, as part of the GPT-4.1 model family launch alongside GPT-4.1 and GPT-4.1 mini. The official model identifier is gpt-4.1-nano-2025-04-14. Multiple official OpenAI sources and the community announcement confirm this release date.

The official model identifier is gpt-4.1-nano-2025-04-14. Multiple official OpenAI sources and the community announcement confirm this release date.

The official model identifier is gpt-4.1-nano-2025-04-14. Multiple official OpenAI sources and the community announcement confirm this release date.

OpenAI officially announced and released o1-mini on September 12, 2024. This was released simultaneously with the o1-preview model. The model was made available to ChatGPT Plus, Team, Enterprise, and Edu users, as well as to tier 5 API users. Multiple official OpenAI sources confirm this date.

OpenAI released o1-pro on March 19, 2025. The model version designation is o1-pro-2025-03-19. Initial rollout began as an exclusive preview for ChatGPT Enterprise users on March 17, 2025, with broader access to Pro and Team subscribers by March 22, 2025, and API availability following shortly after. The date provided (2025-03-19) correctly matches the official announcement and release date.

The o4-mini-high variant was made available exclusively to paid-tier ChatGPT users. The date provided (2025-04-16T00:00:00.000Z) is correct.

DeepSeek-R1-0528 is an upgraded version of the original DeepSeek-R1 released on May 28, 2025. Multiple official sources (Bloomberg, TechCrunch, CNBC) confirm this date. The update improved accuracy from 70% to 87.5% and added JSON output and function calling support.

Date corrected from 2025-07-09T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-07-10T05:28:00.000Z. Grok 4 was officially announced and released on July 9, 2025 at approximately 10:28 PM PDT (2025-07-10T05:28:00.000Z UTC). The currently recorded date of 2025-07-09T00:00:00.000Z is incorrect - it's off by approximately 5.5 hours. The announcement came through xAI and Elon Musk via livestream and social media on X (formerly Twitter).

Date corrected from 2025-10-21T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-07-09T20:00:00.000Z. Grok 4 was officially announced and released on July 9, 2025 at 8 PM PT during an xAI livestream event confirmed by Elon Musk. The provided date of October 21, 2025 is incorrect. Grok 4 was released in July 2025, not October 2025.

Date corrected from 2025-02-19T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-02-17T04:00:00.000Z. Grok 3 was officially released by xAI on February 17, 2025 at 8:00 PM PST (February 18, 2025 at 4:00 AM UTC). The provided date of 2025-02-19T00:00:00.000Z is incorrect—it is two days later than the actual release date. Multiple sources including TechCrunch, CNBC, and Bloomberg confirm the February 17, 2025 release.

Date corrected from 2025-02-19T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-02-17T00:00:00.000Z. Grok 3 and Grok 3 Mini were officially released by xAI on February 17, 2025, as reported by TechCrunch. The recorded date of February 19, 2025 is incorrect by 2 days. The release occurred late on Monday, February 17, 2025.

Date corrected from 2025-02-19T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-03-25T00:00:00.000Z. Gemini 2.5 Pro was first announced and released as an experimental version on March 25, 2025. The public preview with billing support became available on April 4, 2025. The recorded date of 2025-02-19 is incorrect - it predates the actual announcement by over a month.

Date corrected from 2025-02-19T00:00:00.000Z to 2025-03-25T00:00:00.000Z. Gemini 2.5 Pro was first announced and released on March 25, 2025 as 'Gemini 2.5 Pro Experimental' (gemini-2.5-pro-exp-03-25), featuring thinking mode capabilities. The recorded date of February 19, 2025 does not match any official Gemini 2.5 Pro announcement. In February 2025, Google released Gemini 2.0 models (2.0 Flash on Jan 30, 2.0 Pro on Feb 5), but Gemini 2.5 Pro was not announced until March 25, 2025.

o3-Pro was released by OpenAI on June 10, 2025, becoming available to ChatGPT Pro and Team users, with Enterprise and Edu users gaining access the following week. Multiple authoritative sources including TechCrunch and OpenAI developer community confirm this date.

Magistral Small was officially announced by Mistral AI on June 10, 2025. This is the base model's initial release date. While Magistral Small 1.2 was released later in September 2025, the original Magistral Small (version 2506) was released on the recorded date.

Magistral Medium was officially announced by Mistral AI on June 10, 2025. The official blog post confirms this date. The model was released as a preview version in Le Chat and via API on La Plateforme.

Magistral Medium was officially announced by Mistral AI on June 10, 2025. Multiple sources including the official Mistral blog post, TechCrunch, and other tech news outlets confirm this release date. The model was announced as Mistral's first reasoning-focused language model, with Magistral Medium being the enterprise version and Magistral Small being the open-source variant.

GPT-5 Pro was officially announced and released by OpenAI on October 6, 2025, during OpenAI's DevDay 2025 event. The model was introduced as an API offering alongside AgentKit and Sora 2. This is distinct from the base GPT-5 model which was released on August 7, 2025.

The Tiny Recursive Model (TRM) is a 7M-parameter recursive reasoning model developed by Samsung SAIT Montreal and released on October 7, 2025. The model was officially released on GitHub under an MIT license. The date is verified through multiple news sources including MarkTechPost (October 9, 2025), VentureBeat, and Nature. However, note that the developer is Samsung SAIT Montreal, not 'Bespoke' - 'Bespoke AI' refers to Samsung's appliance line, not the model developer.
